slots.title: Operational Excellence Program 2027
slots.subtitle: Standardizing processes, improving efficiency and strengthening cross-functional execution.

slots.action-title: A shared operating model is required to support the next phase of growth.
slots.p1-body: Current operational complexity limits group-wide transparency and slows management decision-making.
slots.p2-body: Operational standards and ways of working differ significantly between regions and acquired entities.
slots.p3-body: Procurement and production processes are not yet harmonized across the business units.
slots.p4-body: Reporting definitions and data quality vary across business units, reducing comparability.
slots.p5-body: The program creates a common operational baseline in process, data and governance.
slots.p6-body: Implementation is phased over three years to manage change and protect ongoing operations.
slots.takeaway: Operational excellence is not a cost-cutting initiative. It is the foundation for scalable growth, transparency and stronger execution across the group.
slots.source: Group Operations, Transformation Office. Illustrative framing.

slots.action-title: Growth through acquisitions has increased operational complexity.
slots.intro: Five years of acquisitive growth have added scale and capability, but also a fragmented operating environment. The figures below describe the current footprint the program must harmonize.
slots.caption: Group manufacturing footprint
slots.s1-l: Production sites across Europe, North America and Asia
slots.source: Group Operations baseline assessment, 2026. Illustrative figures.

slots.action-title: Operational fragmentation creates measurable business impact.
slots.c1-body: Core processes are executed differently across sites, making outcomes hard to predict and to improve.
slots.c2-body: Diverging KPI and cost definitions mean regional figures cannot be compared with confidence.
slots.c3-body: Without a common baseline, decisions that span regions take longer to align and to approve.
slots.c4-body: Local teams independently solve the same problems, duplicating effort and tooling across the group.
slots.c5-body: Fragmented processes do not scale cleanly to new sites, acquisitions or volume increases.

slots.action-title: The program creates a common foundation for efficiency, transparency and execution.
slots.o1d: Common process definitions and standards applied consistently across all sites.
slots.o2d: A single set of KPI definitions so performance is measured the same way everywhere.
slots.o3d: Group-wide spend visibility and consolidated demand to strengthen the negotiating position.
slots.o4d: Eliminate parallel local initiatives and overlapping systems across business units.
slots.o5d: Clear decision rights, ownership and a review cadence for operational standards.
slots.o6d: Reliable, automated reporting that replaces manual consolidation and adjustments.
slots.o7d: Timely, comparable information for faster and better-informed management decisions.
slots.o8d: A mechanism to identify, document and roll out proven improvements between sites.

slots.action-title: The program focuses on five core workstreams.
slots.w1-desc: Consistent process standards and governance across all regions.
slots.w2-desc: One shared performance language and reporting model for the group.
slots.w3-desc: Transparency, consolidation and leverage across global procurement.
slots.w4-desc: Plant-level visibility, standard KPIs and operational discipline.
slots.w5-desc: Digital tools and data that support the standardized processes.

slots.action-title: Creating consistent process standards across regions.
slots.items: Define a global process taxonomy|Identify regional process deviations|Document current-state processes|Align target processes with business owners|Create a process governance model|Establish a review and update cadence
slots.caption: Process mapping workshop

slots.action-title: One shared performance language across the group.
Increasing transparency and leverage across global procurement.
Improving plant-level visibility and operational discipline.
slots.items: Standard production KPIs|Monthly plant performance reviews|OEE tracking by line|Structured downtime categorization|Quality loss tracking|Standard shift handover|Continuous improvement routines

slots.action-title: Digital tools must support standard processes, not replace them.
slots.note: Digital enablement follows process standardization: tools are configured to the agreed target processes, not the other way around.

slots.action-title: Clear decision rights are critical for execution.
slots.l2-role: Runs the program day to day

slots.action-title: The program will be delivered in three phases over three years.
Pilot sites should represent the group's operational diversity.
The program creates financial, operational and organizational benefits.
slots.cat1-items: Improved procurement leverage|Reduced duplicated activities|Better cost transparency
slots.cat2-items: Faster issue identification|More consistent performance reviews|Better best-practice transfer
slots.cat3-items: Clearer ownership and accountability|Improved cross-functional collaboration|Stronger management visibility
slots.takeaway: Benefits compound: transparency enables better decisions, standardization enables scale, and governance keeps both in place.

slots.action-title: Successful implementation requires active change management.
slots.r1-mit: Engage regional leadership early, co-design target processes with site owners, and communicate the rationale.
slots.r2-mit: Run data-quality assessments up front, phase reporting, and assign clear data ownership.
slots.r3-mit: Confirm dedicated capacity per region and protect program roles from day-to-day demands.
slots.r4-mit: Assess the ERP landscape early, align processes to a common target, and sequence technical changes.
slots.r5-mit: Use the steering committee to arbitrate and maintain a single prioritized backlog.
slots.r6-mit: Phase delivery, show early wins, and keep cadence and scope disciplined.

slots.action-title: Management alignment is required before program launch.
slots.forum: These decisions are tabled for sign-off at the Executive Board and Steering Committee review in Q1 2027, ahead of program launch.

slots.closing-line: A common operating model will make the group more transparent, efficient and scalable.
slots.sub: The Operational Excellence Program 2027 establishes the standards, governance and transparency required to support the next phase of profitable growth.
